items.title: Como bid for Chelsea's Chalobah - Sunday's gossip
items.summary: Chelsea are handed a bid from Como for defender Trevoh Chalobah, Everton are frontrunners to sign Blues striker Liam Delap, while Inter Milan may miss out on Liverpool midfielder Curtis Jones over his valuation, plus more.

items.summary: CHRISTIAN FUCHS has made his exit from an Newport County just weeks after guiding them to League Two survival. items.title: Ireland take shock win but West Indies still progress to semi-finals
items.summary: Ireland secured their first win at a Women's T20 World Cup as they beat semi-final hopefuls West Indies by six wickets with 11 balls to spare in Bristol.

items.title: England on brink of defeat, says Broad - but never write off Stokes in chase
items.summary: Stuart Broad believes New Zealand can "put the game to bed" against England on day four of the series-deciding third Test after they opened up a commanding 204-run second-innings lead.

items.summary: ENGLAND players will share a massive £15million bonus if they bring home the World Cup. items.title: Wyatt-Hodge masterclass helps England send New Zealand packing at T20 World Cup
items.summary: Danni Wyatt-Hodge put on a batting masterclass to help England remain unbeaten and send defending champions New Zealand home with a brilliant win at the Kia Oval.

items.title: Gakpo to stay with Netherlands squad after loss of unborn son
items.summary: Cody Gakpo will stay with the Netherlands squad at the World Cup following the loss of his unborn son.

items.title: Why do England have Three Lions on their badge?
items.summary: AS England try to bring football home at the 2026 World Cup, the badge emblazoned on their shirts is one of the most iconic in all sport. But the story behind the Three Lions stretches back well over 800 years, long before the beautiful game even existed. What are the origins of the England badge?..
